formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's the formula sheet for CBSE Class 12 Chemistry — The p-Block Elements (Groups 15–18) in JSON format:

[
  {
    "name": "1. Electron Gain Enthalpy (E.G.E.) Formula",
    "formula": "ΔH = H_(atom) - H_(ion)",
    "variables": "ΔH = Change in Enthalpy, H_(atom) = Enthalpy of Neutral Atom, H_(ion) = Enthalpy of Ion",
    "units": "kJ/mol",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "2. Electron Gain Enthalpy (E.G.E.) Trend Formula",
    "formula": "ΔH_(n) = ΔH_(n-1) + ΔH_(diff)",
    "variables": "ΔH_(n) = Electron Gain Enthalpy of the nth element, ΔH_(n-1) = Electron Gain Enthalpy of the (n-1)th element, ΔH_(diff) = Difference in Electron Gain Enthalpy",
    "units": "kJ/mol",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "3. Ionization Enthalpy (I.E.) Trend Formula",
    "formula": "I.E._{(n+1)} = I.E._n + ΔI.E.",
    "variables": "I.E._{(n+1)} = Ionization Enthalpy of the (n+1)th element, I.E._n = Ionization Enthalpy of the nth element, ΔI.E. = Difference in Ionization Enthalpy",
    "units": "kJ/mol",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "4. Electronegativity Trend Formula",
    "formula": "EN_(n) = EN_(n-1) + ΔEN",
    "variables": "EN_(n) = Electronegativity of the nth element, EN_(n-1) = Electronegativity of the (n-1)th element, ΔEN = Difference in Electronegativity",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "5. Electron Affinity (E.A.) Formula",
    "formula": "E.A. = 1/2 [E_(atom) + E_(ion) - E_(mol)]",
    "variables": "E.A. = Electron Affinity, E_(atom) = Energy of Neutral Atom, E_(ion) = Energy of Ion, E_(mol) = Energy of Molecule",
    "units": "kJ/mol",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "6. Oxidation Potential (E°) Formula",
    "formula": "E°_(cell) = E°_(cathode) - E°_(anode)",
    "variables": "E°_(cell) = Standard Cell Potential, E°_(cathode) = Standard Potential of Cathode, E°_(anode) = Standard Potential of Anode",
    "units": "V",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "7. Oxidation Potential (E°) Trend Formula",
    "formula": "E°_(n+1) = E°_n + ΔE°",
    "variables": "E°_(n+1) = Standard Cell Potential of the (n+1)th element, E°_n = Standard Cell Potential of the nth element, ΔE° = Difference in Standard Cell Potential",
    "units": "V",
    "notes": ""
  }
]

Please note that these formulas are crucial for understanding the properties and trends of p-block elements, but there might be additional formulas specific to certain topics within the chapter.
